contents:
# IRGC stops two ships in Strait of Hormuz to uphold Iranian rights

## Overview
The IRGC identified and stopped two ships in the Strait of Hormuz as part of ongoing enforcement amid U.S.-Iran tensions. This action aligns with Iran's restrictions on non-Iranian shipping since late February in response to U.S. and Israeli military actions.

## Details
The IRGC announcement emphasized upholding the rights of the Iranian nation during the blockade. No specific vessel names remained disclosed in the statement. The incident occurred amid prolonged U.S. naval blockade of Iranian-linked ports ordered by President Trump. Iran has refused to reopen the strait until the U.S. lifts its measures. BBC Verify noted at least four vessels from Iranian ports crossed U.S. lines previously. Tehran warned of targeting approaching vessels.

**Location**: Strait of Hormuz

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @US5thFleet, which covers US 5th Fleet Strait of Hormuz Iran IRGC Houthi Red Sea. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following US 5th Fleet Strait of Hormuz Iran IRGC Houthi Red Sea will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
